# Comparative Named Entity Recognition System
A high-performance research platform comparing CRF and spaCy NER architectures.

## Institutional Context
The project title is Comparative Named Entity Recognition System Using CRF and spaCy Models. This was built for the Artificial Intelligence course in BSCS Semester 3 at ILM College Sargodha, Punjab, Pakistan. Under the mentorship of Sir Abdur-Rehman, the development cycle lasted 8 weeks from 26 January 2026 to 26 March 2026. The system is now fully completed and deployed on AWS-EC2.

## Team Structure
Hassan Ali led the project, managing the CRF lifecycle, backend, and web integration. Mudassir handled the spaCy integration and neural pipeline analysis. Saad Ilyas focused on UI/UX design, moving from the initial Tkinter prototype to the final Flask frontend.

## Technical Evolution
During the first four weeks, we focused on desktop research and developing a functional Tkinter GUI. In weeks five and six, we pivoted to a Flask Web Architecture to better utilize our web technology skills. The final two weeks were dedicated to system integration, AWS-EC2 deployment, and performance benchmarking.

## Key Performance Indicators
Our custom CRF model achieved a 90.32% F1 score after training on 14,041 sentences from the CoNLL2003 dataset. For neural analysis, we used the spaCy en_core_web_sm model which operates with 2.5 billion core parameters.

## Core Documentation
You can find the User Guide at /docs/user and the Developer Manual at /docs/dev. Our full journey is documented at /timeline, and the code is available on GitHub at https://github.com/softdevhassan/COMPARATIVE-NER-NLP-AI.
